lessons.title: Trespass to the Person Battery
lessons.description: What is battery? Battery is the intentional application of direct and unlawful physical force to another person without their consent.

lessons.title: Trespass to the Person Assault and False Imprisonment
lessons.description: Let us begin with assault. In tort law — and it is important to distinguish this from criminal law — assault is the intentional act of causing the plaintiff to apprehend an imminent, unlawful application of force.

lessons.title: Trespass to the Person Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress
lessons.description: The traditional trespass torts — battery, assault, and false imprisonment — protect against specific forms of intentional wrongdoing.
